goal: Create Attack Surface Index (ASI) datamart by business unit with top 5 rankings

marts.sql: CREATE TABLE attack_surface_index_by_business_unit AS SELECT a.business_unit, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) AS total_vulnerabilities, COUNT(DISTINCT m.id) AS total_misconfigurations, COUNT(DISTINCT e.id) AS total_external_exposures, COUNT(DISTINCT ie.id) AS total_identity_exposures FROM assets a LEFT JOIN vulnerabilities v ON a.id = v.asset_id LEFT JOIN misconfigurations m ON a.id = m.asset_id LEFT JOIN external_exposure e ON a.id = e.asset_id LEFT JOIN identity_exposure ie ON a.id = ie.asset_id GROUP BY a.business_unit ORDER BY total_vulnerabilities DESC LIMIT 5;
marts.description: This mart contains the Attack Surface Index (ASI) by business unit, summarizing the total vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, external exposures, and identity exposures.
marts.natural_language_question: Which business units have the highest number of vulnerabilities and exposures?
marts.business_value: This mart helps in understanding the security posture of different business units, enabling targeted resource allocation and risk mitigation strategies.

marts.sql: CREATE TABLE software_asset_management_summary AS SELECT s.department, COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) AS total_software_assets, SUM(s.license_count) AS total_licenses, AVG(s.usage_hours) AS avg_usage_hours FROM software_inventory s GROUP BY s.department ORDER BY total_software_assets DESC LIMIT 5;
marts.description: This mart contains a summary of software assets by department, including total software assets, total licenses, and average usage hours.
marts.natural_language_question: Which departments have the highest number of software assets and how are they utilizing them?
marts.business_value: This mart provides insights into software utilization across departments, aiding in effective license management and resource allocation.

marts.sql: CREATE TABLE vulnerability_risk_assessment AS SELECT v.severity, COUNT(v.id) AS total_vulnerabilities, AVG(v.time_to_remediate) AS avg_time_to_remediate FROM vulnerabilities v GROUP BY v.severity ORDER BY total_vulnerabilities DESC LIMIT 5;
marts.description: This mart contains a risk assessment of vulnerabilities based on their severity, including total vulnerabilities and average time to remediate.
marts.natural_language_question: What are the most common vulnerabilities by severity and how quickly can they be remediated?
marts.business_value: This mart assists in prioritizing remediation efforts based on vulnerability severity, enhancing overall security posture and compliance.
marts.grain: One row per vulnerability severity level

reasoning:
